prose: The frontier orbitals of formaldehyde were computed at the DFT level with the B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (tier 'standard') using the PySCF backend. The calculation was performed in the gas phase using implicit solvation (none) with charge 0 and multiplicity 1. The input geometry was taken directly from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz without further relaxation. The SCF procedure converged, requiring 8 cycles, and the results are considered trustworthy. Key results: the HOMO energy is -7.65231 eV, the LUMO energy is -1.78264 eV, and the HOMO–LUMO gap is calculated as 5.86967 eV. The raw frontier orbitals data (HOMO, HOMO−1, LUMO, etc.) and SCF diagnostics are available in the output files. The exact command was: '/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz --method dft --tier standard --solvent none --charge 0 --mult 1 --stdout json --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpcb79lzwv/live.json'.
